arts.ttl: Exclusive: Aston Villa now in advanced talks to sign £30m player who left Nottingham Forest in 2023
Aston Villa player flying out today to seal transfer – Could be cut-price deal in making
arts.sum: According to a claim in the Turkish media, Aston Villa defender Diego Carlos is on the verge of signing for Fenerbahce, after reports of an agreement between the two clubs. arts.ttl: Monaco 'gut punch' ahead of Aston Villa clash as crisis ensues
arts.sum: Despite being in the respectable position of fourth in Ligue 1, Adi Hutter's side have only won one of their last eight matches in all competitions and that victory came against lower league side Union Saint-Jean in the French Cup. Excluding that routine 4-1 win, Monaco have only claimed victory in one of their last 10. But Monaco fell to a shock 2-1 defeat against Jean-Louis Gasset's Montepellier and Hutter claimed the important match with Villa wasn't in the minds of his players.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa faced with fresh transfer decision as clubs eye loan move
arts.sum: Bogarde has broken onto the first-team scene at Villa this season, having spent two separate loan spells, spanning 12 months, at Bristol Rovers. Should Villa allow Bogarde a loan?

arts.ttl: Aston Villa favourite could join Premier League rivals Man City 'on loan'
arts.sum: Luiz joined Villa from City, originally, in the summer of 2019 following the Lions' promotion back to the Premier League, after the Brazilian struggled to obtain a work permit for the champions. Aston Villa favourite Douglas Luiz could return to the Premier League this month - and rejoin Villa's top four rivals Manchester City on a loan basis.

arts.ttl: ‘Heading for Aston Villa’ – Offer simply too temping and club have to accept
arts.sum: On Sunday, we covered claims from Spain which explained Andrés García’s move to Aston Villa is about to happen for €7m plus €2m bonuses. Levante manager Julián Calero also stated the 21-year-old is “99% out”, indicating his exit will go through soon. arts.ttl: Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
arts.sum: Manchester United’s wing-backs are struggling, Foden is back to his best and Bournemouth are top-four contenders Ruben Amorim is repeatedly learning that his team are worryingly susceptible out wide. Brighton’s first two goals came from players being given space on the flanks in the huge gaps between wing-backs and centre-backs. Kaoru Mitoma gifted Yankuba Minteh a tap in and the favour was repaid in the second half. Leny Yoro had terrible troubles against Southampton and there were similar struggles once again as Brighton tested him on the outside. Noussair Mazraoui had a forgettable afternoon, looking poor in possession, which helped Brighton for the opener and he failed to intervene before Mitoma steered the second home at the back post. Mazraoui was moved to the left and soon the third goal arrived as Yasin Ayari was given the freedom to cross the ball. The wing-back positions need specialists and those available to Amorim are unable to provide the defensive robustness and attacking support required, although he will be sticking with 3-4-3 whether they like it or not. Amorim needs to find a solution. Will Unwin Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa transfer news with latest on Garcia and Bade as trio could leave
arts.sum: Unai Emery has confirmed Diego Carlos could leave Aston Villa in this window with the club in talks over his exit. Villa have a host of existing players who could yet leave the club this month; Diego Carlos, Emiliano Buendia, Louie Barry and Kosta Nedeljkovic are among a host of names who might depart either permanently or on loan.
